Report Summary¶

All key numbers for the paper in one place.

In [70]:
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# REPORT SUMMARY — all numbers for the paper
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Accuracy
display(Markdown("### Accuracy"))
acc_rows = [
    ("n samples",                           n),
    ("Strict correct (DSGym exact_match)",  f"{n_strict}/{n}  ({100*n_strict/max(1,n):.0f}%)"),
    ("LLaMA / Gemini rescued",              f"{n_rescued}/{n}  ({100*n_rescued/max(1,n):.0f}%)"),
    ("Lenient correct (strict + rescued)",  f"{n_lenient}/{n}  ({100*n_lenient/max(1,n):.0f}%)"),
    ("Confirmed wrong",                     f"{n-n_lenient}/{n}  ({100*(n-n_lenient)/max(1,n):.0f}%)"),
]
show_df(pd.DataFrame(acc_rows, columns=["Metric","Value"]).set_index("Metric"))

# Drift taxonomy
display(Markdown("### Drift Taxonomy"))
tax_rows = [(c, cat_counts.get(c,0), f"{100*cat_counts.get(c,0)/max(1,n):.1f}%")
            for c in CAT_ORDER if cat_counts.get(c,0) > 0]
show_df(pd.DataFrame(tax_rows, columns=["Category","N","Pct"]).set_index("Category"))

if not df_drift.empty:
    # Agent divergence
    display(Markdown("### Agent Divergence (O\u2192P)"))
    aligned_   = df_drift["plan_code_alignment"].dropna()
    disagree_  = df_drift["o_p_disagreement"].dropna()
    div_rows = [
        ("Samples with plan extracted",      f"{len(aligned_)}/{len(df_drift)}"),
        ("Mean plan-code alignment",         f"{aligned_.mean():.3f}" if len(aligned_) else "n/a"),
        ("Mean O\u2192P disagreement",         f"{disagree_.mean():.3f}  ({disagree_.mean()*100:.1f}%)" if len(disagree_) else "n/a"),
        ("Nudge trigger rate",               f"{df_drift['nudge_triggered'].mean()*100:.1f}%"),
        ("Nudge recovery rate",              f"{100*n_recovered/max(1,n_nudged):.1f}%" if n_nudged else "n/a"),
    ]
    show_df(pd.DataFrame(div_rows, columns=["Metric","Value"]).set_index("Metric"))

    # Verbal-execution divergence
    display(Markdown("### Verbal-vs-Execution Divergence"))
    hb = df_drift.dropna(subset=["execution_scalar","verbal_scalar"])
    dg = hb[hb["verbal_execution_disagree"]==True]
    vd_rows = [
        ("Samples with both scalars extracted",   len(hb)),
        ("Disagreements",                         f"{len(dg)} ({100*len(dg)/max(1,len(hb)):.1f}%)"),
        ("Disagree + correct (exec won)",         len(dg[dg["correct"]==True])),
        ("Disagree + wrong",                      len(dg[dg["correct"]==False])),
    ]
    show_df(pd.DataFrame(vd_rows, columns=["Metric","Value"]).set_index("Metric"))

    # Drift onset
    display(Markdown("### Drift Onset"))
    onset_rows_ = []
    for lbl, col in [("Turn","drift_onset_turn"),("Tokens","drift_onset_tokens"),("Sec","drift_onset_sec")]:
        vals = df_drift[col].dropna()
        if len(vals):
            onset_rows_.append({"Axis":lbl,"N drifted":len(vals),
                                 "Drift rate":f"{100*len(vals)/max(1,len(df_drift)):.1f}%",
                                 "Mean":round(vals.mean(),2),"Median":round(vals.median(),2),
                                 "Stdev":round(vals.std(),2)})
    if onset_rows_:
        show_df(pd.DataFrame(onset_rows_).set_index("Axis"))

    # Session drift (early vs late)
    display(Markdown("### Session Drift — Early vs Late Half"))
    half = len(df_tl)//2
    early_ = df_tl.iloc[:half]; late_ = df_tl.iloc[half:]
    sess_rows = [
        ("Accuracy — early (samples 1-25)",   f"{early_['correct'].mean():.3f}"),
        ("Accuracy — late  (samples 26-50)",  f"{late_['correct'].mean():.3f}"),
        ("Accuracy \u0394 (late \u2212 early)",     f"{late_['correct'].mean()-early_['correct'].mean():+.3f}"),
        ("Nudge triggers — early / late",     f"{int(early_['nudge_triggered'].sum())} / {int(late_['nudge_triggered'].sum())}"),
    ]
    if has_anchoring:
        sess_rows += [
            ("Orch anchor \u0394 (late \u2212 early)",  f"{late_['orch_anchor'].mean()-early_['orch_anchor'].mean():+.3f}"),
            ("Prog anchor \u0394 (late \u2212 early)",  f"{late_['prog_anchor'].mean()-early_['prog_anchor'].mean():+.3f}"),
            ("Drift gap — early",              f"{early_['orch_anchor'].mean()-early_['prog_anchor'].mean():.3f}"),
            ("Drift gap — late",               f"{late_['orch_anchor'].mean()-late_['prog_anchor'].mean():.3f}"),
        ]
    show_df(pd.DataFrame(sess_rows, columns=["Metric","Value"]).set_index("Metric"))

# Methods section reference
display(Markdown("### Methods Reference"))
impl_rows = [
    ("Benchmark",                  "QRData (DSGym)"),
    ("n samples",                  n),
    ("Nudge mode",                 "targeted (re-injects original question)"),
    ("Max retry turns",            3),
    ("Per-sample timeout",         "300 s"),
    ("Drift threshold",            "DRIFT_RATIO = 0.6 x orchestrator anchoring"),
    ("Token counter",              "tiktoken cl100k_base (whitespace fallback)"),
    ("Scalar tolerance",           "rtol=0.02, atol=1e-6; 100x scale for %"),
    ("Second judge (lenient)",     "LLaMA3 via Ollama (local, no API cost)"),
    ("Solution field to DSGym",    "Extracted scalar as str; fallback = raw HTML"),
    ("Verbal-exec agreement",      "_scalars_agree with rtol=0.02 + percent scaling"),
]
show_df(pd.DataFrame(impl_rows, columns=["Setting","Value"]).set_index("Setting"))

Methods Reference¶

Value
Setting
Benchmark QRData (DSGym)
n samples 50
Nudge mode targeted (re-injects original question)
Max retry turns 3
Per-sample timeout 300 s
Drift threshold DRIFT_RATIO = 0.6 x orchestrator anchoring
Token counter tiktoken cl100k_base (whitespace fallback)
Scalar tolerance rtol=0.02, atol=1e-6; 100x scale for %
Second judge (lenient) LLaMA3 via Ollama (local, no API cost)
Solution field to DSGym Extracted scalar as str; fallback = raw HTML
Verbal-exec agreement _scalars_agree with rtol=0.02 + percent scaling

Task-Intent Drift in LAMBDA — Analysis Notebook¶

Pipeline: main.py → gemini_judge.py → this notebook

Reads evaluation_results/qrdata_lambda_inproc_judged.json.
Drift metadata is embedded in each sample under metadata.drift (re-attached by main.py).
Lenient labels come from judge_result (written by gemini_judge.py).

§ Section
0 Setup & path detection
1 Load & merge — strict / lenient accuracy
2 Drift taxonomy
3 Agent divergence (O→P→Inspector)
4 Drift trajectory
5 Verbal-vs-execution divergence + inspection
6 Nudge recovery
7 Cross-trial consistency
8 Orchestrator vs Programmer accuracy curves
9 Export CSVs & figures
10 Report-ready summary

0 · Setup¶

In [71]:
from __future__ import annotations
import csv, json, re, statistics, warnings
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Dict, Iterable, List, Optional, Tuple

import matplotlib; matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.gridspec as gridspec
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import display, Markdown, Image
from PIL import Image as PILImage
import io
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

# ── Paths: walks up from cwd to find evaluation_results/ ─────────────────────
def _find_dir(name: str) -> Path:
    here = Path.cwd()
    for p in [here, *here.parents]:
        c = p / name
        if c.is_dir(): return c
    return here / name

RESULTS_DIR = _find_dir("evaluation_results")
# Override if needed: RESULTS_DIR = Path("/absolute/path/to/evaluation_results")
JUDGED_PATH = RESULTS_DIR / "qrdata_lambda_inproc_judged.json"
OUT_DIR     = RESULTS_DIR.parent / "drift_analysis"; OUT_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
FIG_DIR     = OUT_DIR / "figures";                   FIG_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

plt.rcParams.update({
    "figure.dpi":130,"axes.spines.top":False,"axes.spines.right":False,
    "axes.grid":True,"grid.alpha":0.25,"font.size":10,
})
CAT_COLORS = {
    "clean":"#4CAF50","early_exit_recovered":"#8BC34A",
    "early_exit_failed":"#F44336","verbal_execution_divergence":"#FF9800",
    "crash":"#9C27B0","other_failure":"#607D8B","unknown":"#BDBDBD",
}
CAT_ORDER = list(CAT_COLORS.keys())

def _to_f64(a):
    return np.array(a, dtype=np.float64)

def _to_float(x):
    try:   return float(x) if x not in (None,"","None") else None
    except: return None

def _show(fig, fname):
    """Save to FIG_DIR and display inline via PIL buffer."""
    out = FIG_DIR / fname
    fig.savefig(out, bbox_inches="tight", dpi=130)
    buf = io.BytesIO()
    fig.savefig(buf, format="png", bbox_inches="tight", dpi=130)
    plt.close(fig)
    buf.seek(0)
    display(Image(data=buf.read(), format="png"))
    print(f"  ✓ {out}")

def _tbl(df, title=""):
    if title: display(Markdown(f"**{title}**"))
    display(df)

print("Setup complete.")
for p in [RESULTS_DIR, JUDGED_PATH]:
    status = "✓" if p.exists() else "✗ NOT FOUND"
    print(f"  {status}  {p}")

Setup complete.
  ✓  /Users/carter/Desktop/Prof_Zheng_Project/evaluation_results
  ✓  /Users/carter/Desktop/Prof_Zheng_Project/evaluation_results/qrdata_lambda_inproc_judged.json

1 · Load & Merge Results¶

In [72]:
# ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def _iter(doc):
    if isinstance(doc, dict):
        for k in ("predictions","samples","results","data"):
            if k in doc and isinstance(doc[k], list):
                yield from doc[k]; return
        if any(k in doc for k in ("metadata","solution","query","ground_truth")):
            yield doc; return
    elif isinstance(doc, list):
        yield from doc

def _sid(s, i): return str(s.get("sample_id") or s.get("id") or s.get("idx") or i)

def _is_strict(s):
    """Mirrors gemini_judge.py is_dsgym_correct exactly."""
    em = (s.get("metrics") or {}).get("exact_match", {})
    if isinstance(em, dict):
        try:   return float(em.get("score", 0)) >= 1.0
        except: return False
    if isinstance(em, (int, float)):
        try:   return float(em) >= 1.0
        except: return False
    return False

def _verdict(s):
    """Reads judge_result (current gemini_judge.py) or gemini_judgment (legacy)."""
    j = s.get("judge_result") or s.get("gemini_judgment")
    if not isinstance(j, dict): return None
    return str(j.get("verdict","")).lower() or None

# ── 1a. Judged JSON (primary — has metrics + judge_result + metadata.drift) ───
judged_list: List[Dict] = []
if JUDGED_PATH.exists():
    with open(JUDGED_PATH) as f: raw = json.load(f)
    judged_list = list(_iter(raw))
    print(f"Judged JSON : {len(judged_list)} samples  ←  {JUDGED_PATH.name}")
else:
    print(f"⚠  {JUDGED_PATH} not found.")
    print("   Run main.py then gemini_judge.py first.")

# ── 1b. Raw results JSON (same data — used as fallback for drift metadata) ────
raw_files = sorted(p for p in RESULTS_DIR.glob("*.json")
                   if "_judged" not in p.name and "_gemini" not in p.name
                   and not p.name.endswith("_metrics.json"))
drift_by_sid: Dict[str, Dict] = {}
run_by_sid:   Dict[str, str]  = {}
for path in raw_files:
    try:
        with open(path) as f: doc = json.load(f)
    except Exception as e: print(f"  skip {path.name}: {e}"); continue
    cfg  = doc.get("config") or {} if isinstance(doc, dict) else {}
    rname = cfg.get("run_name") or (doc.get("run_name") if isinstance(doc,dict) else None)             or path.stem.removesuffix("_results")
    for i, s in enumerate(_iter(doc)):
        sid  = _sid(s, i)
        meta = s.get("metadata") or {}
        if meta.get("drift"): drift_by_sid[sid] = meta
        run_by_sid[sid] = rname
print(f"Raw JSONs   : {len(raw_files)} files, "
      f"drift metadata in {len(drift_by_sid)} samples")

# ── 1c. Merge ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
merged: List[Dict] = []
for i, s in enumerate(judged_list):
    sid     = _sid(s, i)
    strict  = _is_strict(s)
    verdict = _verdict(s)
    rescued = (not strict) and verdict == "correct"
    lenient = strict or rescued
    # metadata.drift comes from the judged JSON itself (main.py re-attached it)
    # drift_by_sid provides a fallback from the raw results file
    meta = s.get("metadata") or drift_by_sid.get(sid) or {}
    merged.append({
        "sample_id":       sid,
        "run_name":        run_by_sid.get(sid, "qrdata_lambda_inproc"),
        "question":        s.get("query") or s.get("question") or "",
        "ground_truth":    str(s.get("ground_truth") or ""),
        "raw_result":      s.get("raw_result") or s.get("solution") or "",
        "strict_correct":  strict,
        "gemini_verdict":  verdict,
        "lenient_correct": lenient,
        "judge_result":    s.get("judge_result") or s.get("gemini_judgment"),
        "metadata":        meta,
        "metrics":         s.get("metrics") or {},
    })

n         = len(merged)
n_strict  = sum(1 for s in merged if s["strict_correct"])
n_rescued = sum(1 for s in merged if s["lenient_correct"] and not s["strict_correct"])
n_lenient = n_strict + n_rescued
n_drift   = sum(1 for s in merged if (s["metadata"] or {}).get("drift"))

print(f"\nMerged      : {n} samples")
print(f"Strict ✓    : {n_strict}/{n}  ({100*n_strict/max(1,n):.0f}%)")
print(f"Rescued ✓   : +{n_rescued}  (LLaMA/Gemini judge)")
print(f"Lenient ✓   : {n_lenient}/{n}  ({100*n_lenient/max(1,n):.0f}%)")
print(f"Drift meta  : {n_drift}/{n} samples instrumented")

Judged JSON : 50 samples  ←  qrdata_lambda_inproc_judged.json
Raw JSONs   : 3 files, drift metadata in 50 samples

Merged      : 50 samples
Strict ✓    : 35/50  (70%)
Rescued ✓   : +15  (LLaMA/Gemini judge)
Lenient ✓   : 50/50  (100%)
Drift meta  : 50/50 samples instrumented

1.1 · Accuracy Summary¶

In [73]:
df_acc = pd.DataFrame([
    ("Total samples",                      n,           ""),
    ("Strict correct (DSGym exact_match)", n_strict,    f"{100*n_strict/max(1,n):.0f}%"),
    ("LLaMA/Gemini rescued",               n_rescued,   f"{100*n_rescued/max(1,n):.0f}%"),
    ("Lenient correct (strict+rescued)",   n_lenient,   f"{100*n_lenient/max(1,n):.0f}%"),
    ("Confirmed wrong",                    n-n_lenient, f"{100*(n-n_lenient)/max(1,n):.0f}%"),
    ("Drift-instrumented",                 n_drift,     f"{100*n_drift/max(1,n):.0f}%"),
], columns=["Metric","Count","Rate"]).set_index("Metric")
_tbl(df_acc, "Accuracy Summary")

Accuracy Summary

Count Rate
Metric
Total samples 50
Strict correct (DSGym exact_match) 35 70%
LLaMA/Gemini rescued 15 30%
Lenient correct (strict+rescued) 50 100%
Confirmed wrong 0 0%
Drift-instrumented 50 100%

2 · Drift Taxonomy¶

Category Definition
clean Correct, no nudge
early_exit_recovered Drifted, nudge recovered
early_exit_failed Drifted, nudge failed
verbal_execution_divergence Prose answer ≠ code output
crash Workflow exception
other_failure Wrong, no early-exit signal
In [74]:
def classify(s):
    d = (s.get("metadata") or {}).get("drift") or {}
    if d.get("crashed"): return "crash"
    if d.get("verbal_execution_disagree") is True: return "verbal_execution_divergence"
    nudged  = bool(d.get("nudge_triggered"))
    correct = s.get("lenient_correct")
    if correct is True  and not nudged: return "clean"
    if correct is True  and nudged:     return "early_exit_recovered"
    if correct is False and nudged:     return "early_exit_failed"
    if correct is False and not nudged: return "other_failure"
    return "unknown"

for s in merged: s["category"] = classify(s)
cat_counts = Counter(s["category"] for s in merged)

df_cats = pd.DataFrame([{
    "Category": c, "Count": cat_counts.get(c,0),
    "Pct (%)": f"{100*cat_counts.get(c,0)/max(1,n):.1f}",
} for c in CAT_ORDER if cat_counts.get(c,0)>0])
_tbl(df_cats, "Drift Category Counts")

Drift Category Counts

Category Count Pct (%)
0 clean 6 12.0
1 early_exit_recovered 23 46.0
2 verbal_execution_divergence 21 42.0
In [75]:
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 4.5))
ax = axes[0]; bottom = 0
for cat in CAT_ORDER:
    val = cat_counts.get(cat,0)
    if not val: continue
    ax.bar(["LAMBDA / QRData"], val, bottom=bottom,
           color=CAT_COLORS[cat], label=f"{cat} ({val})", edgecolor="white")
    if val >= 2:
        ax.text(0, bottom+val/2, f"{cat}\n({val})",
                ha="center", va="center", fontsize=8.5, color="white", fontweight="bold")
    bottom += val
ax.set_ylabel("Samples"); ax.set_title("Drift Category Breakdown")
ax.set_xticks([]); ax.spines["bottom"].set_visible(False)
ax.legend(loc="upper right", fontsize=7.5, bbox_to_anchor=(1.45,1))

ax2 = axes[1]
cats_s = sorted(cat_counts, key=cat_counts.get, reverse=True)
vals   = [cat_counts[c] for c in cats_s]
bars   = ax2.barh(cats_s, vals, color=[CAT_COLORS.get(c,"#888") for c in cats_s], edgecolor="white")
for b, v in zip(bars, vals):
    ax2.text(v+0.3, b.get_y()+b.get_height()/2,
             f"{v}  ({100*v/max(1,n):.0f}%)", va="center", fontsize=9)
ax2.set_xlabel("Samples"); ax2.set_title("Category Distribution")
ax2.set_xlim(0, max(vals+[1])*1.3)
fig.suptitle("Drift Taxonomy — LAMBDA on QRData", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold")
_show(fig, "fig_category_breakdown.png")

  ✓ /Users/carter/Desktop/Prof_Zheng_Project/drift_analysis/figures/fig_category_breakdown.png

3 · Agent Divergence (O → P → Inspector)¶

In [76]:
traj_rows = []   # populated below; initialised here for export cell
drift_rows = []
for s in merged:
    d = (s.get("metadata") or {}).get("drift") or {}
    if not d: continue
    drift_rows.append({
        "sample_id":               s["sample_id"],
        "category":                s["category"],
        "correct":                 s["lenient_correct"],
        "nudge_mode":              d.get("nudge_mode"),
        "trial_id":                d.get("trial_id"),
        "plan_item_count":         len(d.get("plan_items") or []),
        "plan_code_alignment":     _to_float(d.get("plan_code_alignment")),
        "o_p_disagreement":        _to_float(d.get("o_p_disagreement")),
        "nudge_triggered":         bool(d.get("nudge_triggered")),
        "nudge_count":             d.get("nudge_count") or 0,
        "nudge_reason":            ((d.get("nudge_events") or [{}])[0].get("reason")),
        "answer_detected_at_turn": d.get("answer_detected_at_turn"),
        "crashed":                 bool(d.get("crashed")),
        "execution_scalar":        _to_float(d.get("execution_scalar")),
        "verbal_scalar":           _to_float(d.get("verbal_scalar")),
        "verbal_execution_disagree": d.get("verbal_execution_disagree"),
        "drift_onset_turn":        _to_float(d.get("drift_onset_turn")),
        "drift_onset_tokens":      _to_float(d.get("drift_onset_tokens")),
        "drift_onset_sec":         _to_float(d.get("drift_onset_sec")),
        "re_anchor_turn":          _to_float(d.get("re_anchor_turn")),
        "n_turns":                 len(d.get("agent_turns") or []),
        "total_tokens":            _to_float(((d.get("agent_turns") or [{}])[-1]
                                               .get("cumulative_tokens"))),
    })
    for t in (d.get("agent_turns") or []):
        traj_rows.append({
            "sample_id":             s["sample_id"],
            "category":              s["category"],
            "nudge_mode":            d.get("nudge_mode"),
            "trial_id":              d.get("trial_id"),
            "turn_index":            t.get("turn_index"),
            "phase":                 t.get("phase"),
            "elapsed_sec":           _to_float(t.get("elapsed_sec")),
            "phase_sec":             _to_float(t.get("phase_sec")),
            "tokens_in_turn":        _to_float(t.get("tokens_in_turn")),
            "cumulative_tokens":     _to_float(t.get("cumulative_tokens")),
            "task_anchoring":        _to_float(t.get("task_anchoring")),
            "has_code":              bool(t.get("has_code")),
            "has_execution_output":  bool(t.get("has_execution_output")),
            "has_inspector_signals": bool(t.get("has_inspector_signals")),
        })

df_drift = pd.DataFrame(drift_rows) if drift_rows else pd.DataFrame()
df_traj  = pd.DataFrame(traj_rows)  if traj_rows  else pd.DataFrame()
print(f"Samples with drift metadata: {len(drift_rows)} / {n}")
if not df_drift.empty:
    aligned  = df_drift["plan_code_alignment"].dropna()
    disagree = df_drift["o_p_disagreement"].dropna()
    print(f"Mean O→P disagreement    : {disagree.mean():.3f}  ({disagree.mean()*100:.1f}%)")
    print(f"Mean plan-code alignment : {aligned.mean():.3f}")
    print(f"Nudge rate               : {df_drift['nudge_triggered'].mean()*100:.1f}%")
    if not df_traj.empty:
        wf = df_traj[df_traj["phase"]=="workflow"]
        print(f"Inspector signal rate    : {wf['has_inspector_signals'].mean()*100:.1f}%")

Samples with drift metadata: 50 / 50
Mean O→P disagreement    : nan  (nan%)
Mean plan-code alignment : nan
Nudge rate               : 84.0%
Inspector signal rate    : 100.0%
In [77]:
if not df_drift.empty:
    agg = []
    for cat in CAT_ORDER:
        sub = df_drift[df_drift["category"]==cat]
        if sub.empty: continue
        agg.append({
            "Category":           cat, "N": len(sub),
            "O→P disagree":       f"{sub['o_p_disagreement'].mean():.3f}" if sub["o_p_disagreement"].notna().any() else "—",
            "Nudge rate":         f"{sub['nudge_triggered'].mean()*100:.0f}%",
            "Avg turns":          f"{sub['n_turns'].mean():.1f}" if sub["n_turns"].notna().any() else "—",
            "Avg tokens":         f"{sub['total_tokens'].mean():.0f}" if sub["total_tokens"].notna().any() else "—",
        })
    _tbl(pd.DataFrame(agg), "Agent Divergence by Drift Category")
    if not df_traj.empty:
        atbl = df_traj.groupby("phase")["task_anchoring"].agg(Mean="mean",Std="std",N="count").round(3)
        _tbl(atbl, "Mean Task-Anchoring by Agent Phase")

Agent Divergence by Drift Category

Category N O→P disagree Nudge rate Avg turns Avg tokens
0 clean 6 — 0% 2.0 527
1 early_exit_recovered 23 — 100% 3.1 935
2 verbal_execution_divergence 21 — 90% 2.9 842

Mean Task-Anchoring by Agent Phase

Mean Std N
phase
orchestrator 0.000 0.000 50
workflow 0.406 0.114 95
In [78]:
if not df_drift.empty and df_drift["o_p_disagreement"].notna().any():
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4.5))
    vals = df_drift["o_p_disagreement"].dropna()
    axes[0].hist(_to_f64(vals.values), bins=15, color="#1976D2", alpha=0.8, edgecolor="white")
    axes[0].axvline(vals.mean(), color="#E53935", ls="--", lw=1.8, label=f"Mean = {vals.mean():.2f}")
    axes[0].set_xlabel("O→P Disagreement (1 − plan-code alignment)")
    axes[0].set_ylabel("Count"); axes[0].set_title("O→P Disagreement Distribution"); axes[0].legend()
    cat_vals = df_drift.groupby("category")["o_p_disagreement"].mean().dropna().sort_values(ascending=False)
    bars = axes[1].barh(cat_vals.index, _to_f64(cat_vals.values),
                        color=[CAT_COLORS.get(c,"#888") for c in cat_vals.index], alpha=0.85)
    for b,v in zip(bars,cat_vals.values): axes[1].text(v+0.01,b.get_y()+b.get_height()/2,f"{v:.2f}",va="center",fontsize=9)
    axes[1].set_xlabel("Mean O→P Disagreement"); axes[1].set_xlim(0,1.15)
    axes[1].set_title("O→P Disagreement by Drift Category")
    fig.suptitle("Orchestrator–Programmer Divergence", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold")
    _show(fig, "fig_o_p_disagreement.png")

4 · Drift Trajectory¶

In [79]:
if not df_traj.empty and "task_anchoring" in df_traj.columns:
    def _traj_panel(ax, x_col, x_label):
        df = df_traj.dropna(subset=[x_col,"task_anchoring"])
        if df.empty: return
        modes = sorted(df["nudge_mode"].dropna().unique()) or ["all"]
        pal   = plt.cm.tab10(np.linspace(0,1,max(len(modes),1)))
        cmap  = {m:pal[i] for i,m in enumerate(modes)}
        onset_map = {"turn_index":"drift_onset_turn",
                     "cumulative_tokens":"drift_onset_tokens",
                     "elapsed_sec":"drift_onset_sec"}
        for mode in modes:
            sub = df[df["nudge_mode"]==mode] if "nudge_mode" in df.columns else df
            c   = cmap[mode]
            for _,grp in sub.groupby("sample_id"):
                grp=grp.sort_values(x_col)
                ax.plot(_to_f64(grp[x_col].values),_to_f64(grp["task_anchoring"].values),
                        color=c,alpha=0.08,lw=0.8)
            x_all = _to_f64(sub[x_col].dropna().values)
            if not len(x_all): continue
            x_max = x_all[np.isfinite(x_all)].max()
            bins  = np.linspace(0,x_max,11)
            ctrs,mus,sds=[],[],[]
            for lo,hi in zip(bins[:-1],bins[1:]):
                v=sub.loc[(sub[x_col]>=lo)&(sub[x_col]<hi),"task_anchoring"].dropna()
                if len(v): ctrs.append((lo+hi)/2);mus.append(v.mean());sds.append(v.std() if len(v)>1 else 0)
            if ctrs:
                ctrs=_to_f64(ctrs);mus=_to_f64(mus);sds=_to_f64(sds)
                ax.plot(ctrs,mus,color=c,lw=2.5,label=f"{mode} (n={sub['sample_id'].nunique()})")
                ax.fill_between(ctrs,np.clip(mus-sds,0,1),np.clip(mus+sds,0,1),color=c,alpha=0.12)
            onset_col = onset_map.get(x_col)
            if onset_col and drift_rows:
                ov=[_to_float(r.get(onset_col)) for r in drift_rows if _to_float(r.get(onset_col)) is not None]
                if ov:
                    mu=np.mean(ov)
                    ax.axvline(mu,color="#E53935",ls="--",lw=1.3,alpha=0.7)
                    ax.text(mu,0.04,f"  onset\nμ={mu:.1f}",color="#E53935",fontsize=7.5,rotation=90,va="bottom")
        ax.set_xlabel(x_label);ax.set_ylabel("Task-anchoring score")
        ax.set_ylim(-0.02,1.08);ax.legend(fontsize=8)

    fig,axes=plt.subplots(1,3,figsize=(16,4.8))
    _traj_panel(axes[0],"turn_index","Turn index")
    _traj_panel(axes[1],"cumulative_tokens","Cumulative tokens")
    _traj_panel(axes[2],"elapsed_sec","Elapsed seconds")
    for ax,t in zip(axes,["Anchoring vs Turn","Anchoring vs Tokens","Anchoring vs Time (s)"]):
        ax.set_title(t,fontsize=10,fontweight="bold")
    fig.suptitle("Drift Trajectory — Task Anchoring Across Agent Turns",fontsize=12,fontweight="bold")
    _show(fig,"fig_drift_trajectory.png")

    onset_summary={}
    for label,col in [("Turn","drift_onset_turn"),("Tokens","drift_onset_tokens"),("Sec","drift_onset_sec")]:
        vals=[_to_float(r.get(col)) for r in drift_rows if _to_float(r.get(col)) is not None]
        if vals: onset_summary[label]={"n_drifted":len(vals),
            "drift_rate%":f"{100*len(vals)/max(1,len(drift_rows)):.1f}",
            "mean":round(np.mean(vals),2),"median":round(np.median(vals),2),"stdev":round(np.std(vals),2)}
    if onset_summary: _tbl(pd.DataFrame(onset_summary).T,"Drift Onset Statistics")
else:
    print("No trajectory data.")

  ✓ /Users/carter/Desktop/Prof_Zheng_Project/drift_analysis/figures/fig_drift_trajectory.png

5 · Verbal-vs-Execution Divergence¶

LAMBDA produces two numeric signals per sample:

  • Execution scalar — the last bare number printed in the <pre> code-output block
  • Verbal scalar — the number extracted from the prose explanation

These are compared with 2% relative tolerance (and 100×/0.01× scaling for percentage encoding).
Disagreement = they differ beyond that tolerance.

In [80]:
if not df_drift.empty:
    has_both = df_drift.dropna(subset=["execution_scalar","verbal_scalar"])
    disagree = has_both[has_both["verbal_execution_disagree"]==True]
    n_both,n_dis = len(has_both),len(disagree)
    print(f"Samples with both scalars extracted : {n_both} / {len(drift_rows)}")
    print(f"Disagreements                       : {n_dis} ({100*n_dis/max(1,n_both):.1f}%)")
    if n_dis:
        exec_won   = disagree[disagree["correct"]==True]
        both_wrong = disagree[disagree["correct"]==False]
        print(f"  Disagree + lenient-correct (exec won) : {len(exec_won)}")
        print(f"  Disagree + wrong (both wrong)          : {len(both_wrong)}")

    div_summary = pd.DataFrame([
        ("Samples with both scalars",       n_both,  ""),
        ("Disagreements",                   n_dis,   f"{100*n_dis/max(1,n_both):.1f}%"),
        ("Disagree & correct (exec won)",   len(exec_won) if n_dis else 0,  ""),
        ("Disagree & wrong (both failed)",  len(both_wrong) if n_dis else 0,""),
    ],columns=["Metric","Count","Rate"]).set_index("Metric")
    _tbl(div_summary,"Verbal-vs-Execution Summary")
else:
    print("No drift data.")

  Disagree + wrong (both wrong)          : 0

In [81]:
if not df_drift.empty:
    has_both  = df_drift.dropna(subset=["execution_scalar","verbal_scalar"])
    disagree  = has_both[has_both["verbal_execution_disagree"] == True]
    n_both, n_dis = len(has_both), len(disagree)
    exec_won   = disagree[disagree["correct"] == True]  if n_dis else pd.DataFrame()
    both_wrong = disagree[disagree["correct"] == False] if n_dis else pd.DataFrame()

    print(f"Samples with both scalars : {n_both}")
    print(f"Disagreements             : {n_dis} ({100*n_dis/max(1,n_both):.1f}%)")
    if n_dis:
        print(f"  Disagree + correct (execution won) : {len(exec_won)}")
        print(f"  Disagree + wrong (both wrong)      : {len(both_wrong)}")

    div_tbl = pd.DataFrame([
        {"Metric":"Samples with both scalars",        "Value": n_both},
        {"Metric":"Disagreements",                    "Value": f"{n_dis} ({100*n_dis/max(1,n_both):.1f}%)"},
        {"Metric":"Disagree + correct (exec won)",    "Value": len(exec_won)},
        {"Metric":"Disagree + wrong (both fail)",     "Value": len(both_wrong)},
    ]).set_index("Metric")
    show_df(div_tbl, "Verbal-vs-Execution Summary")

    if n_both > 0:
        fig = plt.figure(figsize=(16, 5))
        ax_pie  = fig.add_subplot(1, 3, 1)
        ax_full = fig.add_subplot(1, 3, 2)
        ax_zoom = fig.add_subplot(1, 3, 3)

        # Pie
        ax_pie.pie(
            [n_both - n_dis, n_dis],
            labels=[f"Agree ({n_both-n_dis})", f"Disagree ({n_dis})"],
            colors=["#4CAF50","#F44336"], autopct="%1.0f%%", startangle=90,
            wedgeprops=dict(edgecolor="white", lw=1.5),
        )
        ax_pie.set_title("Agreement / Disagreement")

        ex = _to_f64(has_both["execution_scalar"].values)
        ve = _to_f64(has_both["verbal_scalar"].values)
        c_map = has_both["verbal_execution_disagree"].map(
            {True:"#F44336", False:"#4CAF50", None:"#9E9E9E"}).fillna("#9E9E9E")

        # Full-range scatter
        ax_full.scatter(ex, ve, c=c_map, alpha=0.65, s=55, edgecolors="white", lw=0.5, zorder=3)
        lo = min(ex.min(), ve.min()); hi = max(ex.max(), ve.max())
        ax_full.plot([lo,hi],[lo,hi],"k--",alpha=0.25,label="Perfect agreement")
        ax_full.scatter([],[],c="#F44336",s=40,label="Disagree")
        ax_full.scatter([],[],c="#4CAF50",s=40,label="Agree")
        ax_full.set_xlabel("Execution scalar"); ax_full.set_ylabel("Verbal scalar")
        ax_full.set_title("Full range"); ax_full.legend(fontsize=8)

        # Zoomed scatter — proportions and p-values (|value| <= 5)
        zoom_mask = (has_both["execution_scalar"].abs() <= 5) & (has_both["verbal_scalar"].abs() <= 5)
        hz = has_both[zoom_mask]
        if len(hz):
            c_z = hz["verbal_execution_disagree"].map(
                {True:"#F44336", False:"#4CAF50", None:"#9E9E9E"}).fillna("#9E9E9E")
            exz = _to_f64(hz["execution_scalar"].values)
            vez = _to_f64(hz["verbal_scalar"].values)
            ax_zoom.scatter(exz, vez, c=c_z, alpha=0.75, s=65, edgecolors="white", lw=0.5, zorder=3)
            ax_zoom.plot([0,5],[0,5],"k--",alpha=0.25,label="Perfect agreement")
            n_dis_zoom = int(zoom_mask.sum())
            ax_zoom.scatter([],[],c="#F44336",s=40,label=f"Disagree ({n_dis_zoom} visible)")
            ax_zoom.scatter([],[],c="#4CAF50",s=40,label="Agree")
            ax_zoom.set_xlabel("Execution scalar"); ax_zoom.set_ylabel("Verbal scalar")
            ax_zoom.set_title("Zoomed: |value| <= 5\n(proportions, p-values)")
            ax_zoom.legend(fontsize=8)
            ax_zoom.set_xlim(-0.1, 5.1); ax_zoom.set_ylim(-0.1, 5.1)
        else:
            ax_zoom.text(0.5,0.5,"No small-value samples",ha="center",va="center",
                         transform=ax_zoom.transAxes, color="#aaa")
            ax_zoom.set_title("Zoomed: |value| <= 5")

        fig.suptitle("Verbal-vs-Execution Divergence", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold")
        show_fig(fig, "fig_verbal_vs_execution.png")

    # Diagnostic: print disagreements for manual validation
    if n_dis:
        id_to_q  = {s["sample_id"]: s["question"]     for s in merged}
        id_to_gt = {s["sample_id"]: s["ground_truth"] for s in merged}
        diag = disagree[["sample_id","execution_scalar","verbal_scalar","correct"]].copy()
        diag["question"]     = diag["sample_id"].map(id_to_q).str[:65]
        diag["ground_truth"] = diag["sample_id"].map(id_to_gt)
        diag["outcome"]      = diag["correct"].map({True:"code right, prose wrong",
                                                     False:"both wrong or prose closer", None:"?"})
        out = diag[["question","ground_truth","execution_scalar","verbal_scalar","outcome"]]
        show_df(out.reset_index(drop=True), f"All {n_dis} Disagreements — Manual Validation")
        display(Markdown(
            "> **Reading this table:** `execution_scalar` = last number the code printed. "
            "`verbal_scalar` = number extracted from prose. "
            "outcome='code right, prose wrong' = genuine verbal-execution divergence. "
            "If neither matches `ground_truth`, it is a real failure independent of divergence."
        ))
else:
    print("No drift data available.")

Reading this table: execution_scalar = last number the code printed. verbal_scalar = number extracted from prose. outcome='code right, prose wrong' = genuine verbal-execution divergence. If neither matches ground_truth, it is a real failure independent of divergence.

5.1 · Disagreement Inspection Table¶

Manual check: genuine divergence vs extraction artifact.

In [82]:
if not df_drift.empty and n_dis > 0:
    id_to_q  = {s["sample_id"]: s["question"]      for s in merged}
    id_to_gt = {s["sample_id"]: s["ground_truth"]  for s in merged}
    insp = disagree[["sample_id","execution_scalar","verbal_scalar","correct"]].copy()
    insp["question"]      = insp["sample_id"].map(id_to_q)
    insp["ground_truth"]  = insp["sample_id"].map(id_to_gt)
    insp["exec_correct?"] = insp.apply(
        lambda r: abs(r["execution_scalar"] - float(r["ground_truth"])) <
                  0.03 * max(abs(r["execution_scalar"]), abs(float(r["ground_truth"])), 1e-9)
                  if r["ground_truth"].replace(".","").replace("-","").isdigit() else "—", axis=1
    )
    display_cols = ["question","ground_truth","execution_scalar","verbal_scalar","correct","exec_correct?"]
    pd.set_option("display.max_colwidth", 80)
    _tbl(insp[display_cols].reset_index(drop=True), "All Disagreement Cases")
    print("\nNote: 'exec_correct?' = True means execution scalar matched ground truth")
    print("      even when the prose said something different.")
else:
    print("No disagreements to inspect.")

All Disagreement Cases

      even when the prose said something different.

6 · Nudge Recovery Analysis¶

In [83]:
nudged = [s for s in merged if (s.get("metadata") or {}).get("drift",{}).get("nudge_triggered")]
n_nudged    = len(nudged)
n_recovered = sum(1 for s in nudged if s["category"]=="early_exit_recovered")
n_failed    = sum(1 for s in nudged if s["category"]=="early_exit_failed")
print(f"Nudged          : {n_nudged}/{n}  ({100*n_nudged/max(1,n):.1f}%)")
print(f"  Recovered     : {n_recovered}  ({100*n_recovered/max(1,n_nudged):.1f}%)")
print(f"  Failed        : {n_failed}  ({100*n_failed/max(1,n_nudged):.1f}%)")

by_reason: Dict[str,Dict[str,int]] = defaultdict(lambda:{"nudged":0,"recovered":0})
for s in nudged:
    d    = (s.get("metadata") or {}).get("drift") or {}
    evts = d.get("nudge_events") or []
    r    = evts[0].get("reason","unknown") if evts else "unknown"
    by_reason[r]["nudged"]    += 1
    if s["category"]=="early_exit_recovered": by_reason[r]["recovered"] += 1

df_rec = pd.DataFrame([{"Trigger":r,"Nudged":v["nudged"],"Recovered":v["recovered"],
    "Rate":f"{100*v['recovered']/max(1,v['nudged']):.0f}%"}
    for r,v in sorted(by_reason.items())])
if not df_rec.empty:
    df_rec = pd.concat([df_rec,
        pd.DataFrame([{"Trigger":"TOTAL","Nudged":n_nudged,"Recovered":n_recovered,
                        "Rate":f"{100*n_recovered/max(1,n_nudged):.0f}%"}])],
        ignore_index=True)
_tbl(df_rec,"Recovery Rate by Trigger Reason")

Nudged          : 42/50  (84.0%)
  Recovered     : 23  (54.8%)
  Failed        : 0  (0.0%)

Recovery Rate by Trigger Reason

Trigger Nudged Recovered Rate
0 ends_with_next_steps 42 23 55%
1 TOTAL 42 23 55%
In [84]:
if n_nudged > 0 and not df_rec.empty:
    df_r = df_rec[df_rec["Trigger"]!="TOTAL"].copy()
    df_r["Nudged_n"]    = pd.to_numeric(df_r["Nudged"],errors="coerce").fillna(0)
    df_r["Recovered_n"] = pd.to_numeric(df_r["Recovered"],errors="coerce").fillna(0)
    df_r["Rate_f"]      = df_r["Recovered_n"]/df_r["Nudged_n"].replace(0,np.nan)
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9,4))
    x = np.arange(len(df_r)); w = 0.32
    b1 = ax.bar(x-w/2,_to_f64(df_r["Nudged_n"].values),    w,label="Nudged",    color="#1976D2",alpha=0.85)
    b2 = ax.bar(x+w/2,_to_f64(df_r["Recovered_n"].values), w,label="Recovered", color="#4CAF50",alpha=0.85)
    for bars in [b1,b2]:
        for b in bars:
            if b.get_height()>0: ax.text(b.get_x()+b.get_width()/2,b.get_height()+0.1,
                                         str(int(b.get_height())),ha="center",fontsize=9)
    ax2=ax.twinx()
    ax2.plot(x,_to_f64(df_r["Rate_f"].fillna(0).values),"D--",color="#E53935",ms=8,lw=1.8,label="Recovery rate")
    ax2.set_ylim(0,1.25); ax2.set_ylabel("Recovery rate",color="#E53935"); ax2.tick_params(axis="y",colors="#E53935")
    ax.set_xticks(x); ax.set_xticklabels(df_r["Trigger"],rotation=12,ha="right")
    ax.set_ylabel("Samples"); ax.set_title("Nudge Recovery by Trigger Reason",fontweight="bold")
    h1,l1=ax.get_legend_handles_labels(); h2,l2=ax2.get_legend_handles_labels()
    ax.legend(h1+h2,l1+l2,fontsize=8)
    _show(fig,"fig_recovery_by_reason.png")

  ✓ /Users/carter/Desktop/Prof_Zheng_Project/drift_analysis/figures/fig_recovery_by_reason.png

7 · Cross-Trial Consistency¶

In [85]:
by_sample: Dict[str,List] = defaultdict(list)
for s in merged:
    d    = (s.get("metadata") or {}).get("drift") or {}
    mode = d.get("nudge_mode") or "unknown"
    by_sample[f"{s['sample_id']}::{mode}"].append(s)

cons_rows=[]
for key,trials in by_sample.items():
    if len(trials)<2: continue
    sid,mode=key.rsplit("::",1)
    cats=sorted({t["category"] for t in trials})
    n_c=sum(1 for t in trials if t.get("lenient_correct") is True)
    cons_rows.append({"sample_id":sid,"nudge_mode":mode,"n_trials":len(trials),
        "n_correct":n_c,"frac_correct":round(n_c/len(trials),2),
        "categories":"|".join(cats),"stable":len(cats)==1,
        "always_fails":n_c==0,"always_succeeds":n_c==len(trials)})

if cons_rows:
    df_cons=pd.DataFrame(cons_rows)
    sf=df_cons[df_cons["always_fails"]]; sp=df_cons[df_cons["always_succeeds"]]
    _tbl(pd.DataFrame([
        ("Pairs with ≥2 trials",              len(df_cons), ""),
        ("Always fail (structural drift)",    len(sf),      f"{100*len(sf)/max(1,len(df_cons)):.0f}%"),
        ("Always succeed (stable clean)",     len(sp),      f"{100*len(sp)/max(1,len(df_cons)):.0f}%"),
        ("Mixed / stochastic",                len(df_cons)-len(sf)-len(sp), ""),
    ],columns=["Type","Count","Pct"]).set_index("Type"), "Cross-Trial Consistency")
    if len(sf): _tbl(sf[["sample_id","n_trials","categories"]].head(10),"Structural Failure Candidates")
else:
    print("Need ≥2 trials. Set N_TRIALS=3 in main.py and re-run.")

Need ≥2 trials. Set N_TRIALS=3 in main.py and re-run.

8 · Orchestrator vs Programmer Accuracy Curves¶

Overfitting-style dual-line graph testing three hypotheses about drift.

Panel X-axis Hypothesis
A (full-width) Sample index 1→50 Session contamination: programmer falls, orchestrator stays stable
B (bottom-left) Turn index Within-sample U-shape: drop at handoff, recovery after nudge
C (bottom-right) Tokens / response length Complexity penalty: heavier samples → more drift
In [86]:
_TL_COLS = ["sample_idx","sample_id","correct","strict_correct","orch_anchor",
            "prog_anchor","anchor_drop","op_disagree","total_tokens","nudge_triggered","drift_onset_turn"]

def _rolling(arr, w=7):
    arr=np.array(arr,dtype=float); out=np.full_like(arr,np.nan)
    for i in range(len(arr)):
        lo=max(0,i-w//2); hi=min(len(arr),i+w//2+1)
        v=arr[lo:hi][~np.isnan(arr[lo:hi])]
        out[i]=v.mean() if len(v) else np.nan
    return out

sample_timeline=[]
for idx,s in enumerate(merged):
    d=    (s.get("metadata") or {}).get("drift") or {}
    turns=(d.get("agent_turns") or [])
    ot=[t for t in turns if t.get("phase")=="orchestrator"]
    pt=[t for t in turns if t.get("phase")=="workflow"]
    pa=[_to_float(t.get("task_anchoring")) for t in pt]
    oa=_to_float(ot[0].get("task_anchoring")) if ot else None
    pf=[v for v in pa if v is not None]
    sample_timeline.append({
        "sample_idx":idx+1,"sample_id":s["sample_id"],
        "correct":1 if s["lenient_correct"] else 0,
        "strict_correct":1 if s["strict_correct"] else 0,
        "orch_anchor":oa,
        "prog_anchor":float(np.mean(pf)) if pf else None,
        "anchor_drop":(float(oa)-float(np.mean(pf))) if oa is not None and pf else None,
        "op_disagree":_to_float(d.get("o_p_disagreement")),
        "total_tokens":_to_float(turns[-1].get("cumulative_tokens") if turns else None),
        "nudge_triggered":1 if d.get("nudge_triggered") else 0,
        "drift_onset_turn":_to_float(d.get("drift_onset_turn")),
    })
if sample_timeline:
    df_tl=pd.DataFrame(sample_timeline)
    for c in _TL_COLS:
        if c not in df_tl.columns: df_tl[c]=None
else:
    df_tl=pd.DataFrame(columns=_TL_COLS)

has_anch=("orch_anchor" in df_tl.columns and df_tl["orch_anchor"].notna().any())

# Drift-gap statistics
if not df_tl.empty:
    half=len(df_tl)//2
    early=df_tl.iloc[:half]; late=df_tl.iloc[half:]
    tbl=[("Accuracy early half",f"{early['correct'].mean():.3f}"),
         ("Accuracy late half", f"{late['correct'].mean():.3f}"),
         ("Accuracy Δ",         f"{late['correct'].mean()-early['correct'].mean():+.3f}"),]
    if has_anch:
        tbl+=[("Orch anchor early",f"{early['orch_anchor'].mean():.3f}"),
              ("Orch anchor late", f"{late['orch_anchor'].mean():.3f}"),
              ("Prog anchor early",f"{early['prog_anchor'].mean():.3f}"),
              ("Prog anchor late", f"{late['prog_anchor'].mean():.3f}"),
              ("Drift gap early",  f"{early['orch_anchor'].mean()-early['prog_anchor'].mean():.3f}"),
              ("Drift gap late",   f"{late['orch_anchor'].mean()-late['prog_anchor'].mean():.3f}"),]
    if df_tl["op_disagree"].notna().any():
        r=df_tl[["op_disagree","correct"]].dropna().corr().iloc[0,1]
        tbl.append(("O→P disagree ↔ accuracy (r)",f"{r:.3f}"))
    tbl.append(("Nudge triggers early/late",
                f"{int(df_tl.iloc[:half]['nudge_triggered'].sum())} / "
                f"{int(df_tl.iloc[half:]['nudge_triggered'].sum())}"))
    _tbl(pd.DataFrame(tbl,columns=["Metric","Value"]).set_index("Metric"),
         "Session-Level Drift Gap Statistics")

Session-Level Drift Gap Statistics

Value
Metric
Accuracy early half 1.000
Accuracy late half 1.000
Accuracy Δ +0.000
Orch anchor early 0.000
Orch anchor late 0.000
Prog anchor early 0.406
Prog anchor late 0.442
Drift gap early -0.406
Drift gap late -0.442
Nudge triggers early/late 20 / 22
In [87]:
fig=plt.figure(figsize=(16,12))
gs=gridspec.GridSpec(2,2,figure=fig,hspace=0.42,wspace=0.32)
ROLL_W=7

# Panel A
ax_a=fig.add_subplot(gs[0,:])
x=_to_f64(df_tl["sample_idx"].values)
acc_roll=_to_f64(_rolling(df_tl["correct"].values,ROLL_W))
ax_a.plot(x,acc_roll,color="#E53935",lw=2.5,zorder=3,label="Programmer outcome — rolling accuracy")
ax_a.fill_between(x,acc_roll,_to_f64(np.zeros_like(acc_roll)),alpha=0.08,color="#E53935")
if has_anch:
    or_=_to_f64(_rolling(df_tl["orch_anchor"].ffill().values,ROLL_W))
    pr_=_to_f64(_rolling(df_tl["prog_anchor"].ffill().values,ROLL_W))
    ax_a.plot(x,or_,color="#1976D2",lw=2.5,zorder=3,label="Orchestrator intent capture — rolling anchoring")
    ax_a.plot(x,pr_,color="#FB8C00",lw=2.0,ls="--",zorder=3,label="Programmer anchoring — rolling")
    ax_a.fill_between(x,_to_f64(np.minimum(or_,pr_)),_to_f64(np.maximum(or_,pr_)),
                      color="#9C27B0",alpha=0.10,label="Drift gap (O–P)")
elif df_tl["op_disagree"].notna().any():
    op_=_to_f64(_rolling(df_tl["op_disagree"].ffill().values,ROLL_W))
    ax_a.plot(x,1.0-op_,color="#1976D2",lw=2.5,label="O–P alignment (1−disagree)")
    ax_a.text(0.5,0.93,"⚠  Full anchoring needs instrumented wrapper",
              transform=ax_a.transAxes,ha="center",fontsize=8.5,color="#777",
              bbox=dict(boxstyle="round,pad=0.3",fc="lightyellow",ec="#ccc"))
nx=df_tl.loc[df_tl["nudge_triggered"]==1,"sample_idx"].values
for i_nx,nxx in enumerate(nx):
    ax_a.axvline(nxx,color="#9C27B0",alpha=0.18,lw=1.0,zorder=1,
                 label=f"Nudge events (n={len(nx)})" if i_nx==0 else "")
ax_a.set_xlim(1,max(len(df_tl),2)); ax_a.set_ylim(-0.05,1.12)
ax_a.set_xlabel("Sample index (evaluation order)",fontsize=11)
ax_a.set_ylabel("Score (0–1)",fontsize=11)
ax_a.set_title("Panel A — Session-Level Drift: Orchestrator vs Programmer  (← train/test accuracy analogy)",
               fontsize=11,fontweight="bold")
ax_a.legend(loc="lower left",fontsize=8.5,framealpha=0.9)
ax_a.text(0.01,0.97,"Hypothesis: programmer line falls as x increases; orchestrator stays stable.",
          transform=ax_a.transAxes,fontsize=8.5,va="top",color="#444",
          bbox=dict(boxstyle="round,pad=0.3",fc="white",ec="#ddd",alpha=0.85))

# Panel B
ax_b=fig.add_subplot(gs[1,0])
if not df_traj.empty and "task_anchoring" in df_traj.columns:
    sc={s["sample_id"]:s["lenient_correct"] for s in merged}
    for sid,grp in df_traj.groupby("sample_id"):
        grp=grp.sort_values("turn_index")
        ax_b.plot(_to_f64(grp["turn_index"].values),_to_f64(grp["task_anchoring"].values),
                  color="#4CAF50" if sc.get(sid) else "#F44336",alpha=0.13,lw=0.9)
    grpd=df_traj.groupby("turn_index")["task_anchoring"]
    mv=_to_f64(grpd.mean().values); sv=_to_f64(grpd.std().fillna(0).values); ti=_to_f64(grpd.mean().index.values)
    ax_b.plot(ti,mv,color="#1A237E",lw=2.5,marker="o",ms=7,label="Mean ±std",zorder=4)
    ax_b.fill_between(ti,np.clip(mv-sv,0,1),np.clip(mv+sv,0,1),color="#1A237E",alpha=0.10)
    ax_b.axvspan(-0.4,0.4,color="#1976D2",alpha=0.07,label="Orchestrator (turn 0)")
    mt=int(df_traj["turn_index"].max())
    if mt>0: ax_b.axvspan(0.6,mt+0.4,color="#FB8C00",alpha=0.05,label="Programmer turns")
    ax_b.plot([],[],color="#4CAF50",alpha=0.6,lw=1.5,label="Correct"); ax_b.plot([],[],color="#F44336",alpha=0.6,lw=1.5,label="Wrong")
    ax_b.legend(fontsize=7.5,loc="upper right"); ax_b.set_xlabel("Turn index (0=orchestrator)",fontsize=10)
else:
    ax_b.text(0.5,0.5,"No per-turn data.",ha="center",va="center",transform=ax_b.transAxes,color="#aaa",fontsize=10)
ax_b.set_ylim(-0.02,1.08); ax_b.set_ylabel("Task-anchoring score",fontsize=10)
ax_b.set_title("Panel B — Within-Sample Turn Trajectory\n(U-shape: drop at handoff, recovery after nudge)",fontsize=10,fontweight="bold")

# Panel C
ax_c=fig.add_subplot(gs[1,1])
has_tok=df_tl["total_tokens"].notna().sum()>=5
if has_tok: xv=_to_f64(df_tl["total_tokens"].values); xl="Total tokens"
else: xv=_to_f64([len(s.get("raw_result","")) for s in merged]); xl="Response length (chars)"
c01=_to_f64(df_tl["correct"].values); cc=np.where(c01==1,"#4CAF50","#F44336")
jit=np.random.default_rng(0).uniform(-0.025,0.025,len(c01))
ax_c.scatter(xv,c01+jit,c=cc,alpha=0.55,s=45,edgecolors="white",lw=0.5,zorder=3)
valid=np.isfinite(xv)
if valid.sum()>=10:
    try:
        _df=pd.DataFrame({"x":xv[valid],"y":c01[valid]})
        _df["bin"]=pd.qcut(_df["x"],q=5,duplicates="drop")
        _bs=_df.groupby("bin",observed=True)["y"].agg(["mean","sem"])
        _ct=_to_f64([iv.mid for iv in _bs.index]); _mu=_to_f64(_bs["mean"].values); _se=_to_f64(_bs["sem"].fillna(0).values)
        ax_c.plot(_ct,_mu,"D-",color="#1A237E",ms=9,lw=2.5,label="Bin mean accuracy",zorder=5)
        ax_c.fill_between(_ct,np.clip(_mu-_se,0,1),np.clip(_mu+_se,0,1),color="#1A237E",alpha=0.12)
    except Exception as _e: ax_c.text(0.5,0.5,f"Binning: {_e}",ha="center",va="center",transform=ax_c.transAxes,fontsize=8)
ax_c.set_xlabel(xl,fontsize=10); ax_c.set_ylabel("Correct (1) / Wrong (0)",fontsize=10)
ax_c.set_yticks([0,1]); ax_c.set_yticklabels(["Wrong","Correct"]); ax_c.legend(fontsize=8)
ax_c.set_title("Panel C — Complexity Penalty\n(heavier samples → more drift → lower accuracy)",fontsize=10,fontweight="bold")

fig.suptitle("Task-Intent Drift in LAMBDA — Orchestrator vs Programmer Accuracy\nLAMBDA / QRData (DSGym), n=50",
             fontsize=13,fontweight="bold",y=1.01)
_show(fig,"fig_drift_accuracy_curves.png")

  ✓ /Users/carter/Desktop/Prof_Zheng_Project/drift_analysis/figures/fig_drift_accuracy_curves.png

10 · Export CSVs & Figures¶

In [88]:
def _wcsv(path, rows_or_df, label=""):
    if isinstance(rows_or_df, pd.DataFrame):
        df_=rows_or_df.reset_index() if rows_or_df.index.name else rows_or_df
        df_.to_csv(path,index=False); n_=len(df_)
    elif rows_or_df:
        keys=list(dict.fromkeys(k for r in rows_or_df for k in r))
        with open(path,"w",newline="") as f:
            w=csv.DictWriter(f,fieldnames=keys,extrasaction="ignore")
            w.writeheader(); [w.writerow(r) for r in rows_or_df]
        n_=len(rows_or_df)
    else: path.write_text(""); n_=0
    print(f"  ✓ {path.name}  [{n_} rows]" + (f"  ({label})" if label else ""))

print(f"CSVs → {OUT_DIR}/")
_SKIP={"agent_turns","plan_items","code_actions","nudge_events","orchestrator_intent","execution_output","verbal_text"}
_wcsv(OUT_DIR/"per_sample.csv",[{
    "sample_id":s["sample_id"],"run_name":s["run_name"],"question":s["question"],
    "ground_truth":s["ground_truth"],"strict_correct":s["strict_correct"],
    "lenient_correct":s["lenient_correct"],"gemini_verdict":s["gemini_verdict"],
    "category":s["category"],
    **{k:v for k,v in ((s.get("metadata") or {}).get("drift") or {}).items() if k not in _SKIP},
} for s in merged],"one row per sample")
if traj_rows:     _wcsv(OUT_DIR/"drift_trajectory.csv",traj_rows,"per-turn anchoring")
if sample_timeline: _wcsv(OUT_DIR/"session_timeline.csv",sample_timeline,"Panel A data")
if drift_rows:    _wcsv(OUT_DIR/"drift_rows.csv",drift_rows,"per-sample drift signals")
_wcsv(OUT_DIR/"category_counts.csv",
      pd.DataFrame([{"category":c,"count":cat_counts.get(c,0),"pct":round(100*cat_counts.get(c,0)/max(1,n),1)}
                    for c in CAT_ORDER]),"taxonomy")
if not df_rec.empty: _wcsv(OUT_DIR/"recovery_rate.csv",df_rec,"nudge recovery")
if cons_rows:     _wcsv(OUT_DIR/"consistency.csv",pd.DataFrame(cons_rows),"cross-trial")
print(f"\nFigures → {FIG_DIR}/")
for p in sorted(FIG_DIR.glob("*.png")): print(f"  ✓ {p.name}")
print("\nExport done ✓")

CSVs → /Users/carter/Desktop/Prof_Zheng_Project/drift_analysis/

  ✓ session_timeline.csv  [50 rows]  (Panel A data)
  ✓ drift_rows.csv  [50 rows]  (per-sample drift signals)

  ✓ recovery_rate.csv  [2 rows]  (nudge recovery)

10 · Report-Ready Summary¶

Key numbers for the paper, collected in one place.

In [89]:
display(Markdown("### Accuracy"))
display(Markdown(f"""
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Total samples | **{n}** |
| Strict correct (DSGym exact_match) | **{n_strict}/{n} ({100*n_strict/max(1,n):.0f}%)** |
| LLaMA/Gemini rescued | **+{n_rescued}** |
| Lenient correct | **{n_lenient}/{n} ({100*n_lenient/max(1,n):.0f}%)** |
| Confirmed wrong | **{n-n_lenient}/{n} ({100*(n-n_lenient)/max(1,n):.0f}%)** |
"""))

display(Markdown("### Drift Taxonomy"))
rows_=[(c,cat_counts.get(c,0),f"{100*cat_counts.get(c,0)/max(1,n):.1f}%") for c in CAT_ORDER if cat_counts.get(c,0)]
display(pd.DataFrame(rows_,columns=["Category","Count","Pct"]).set_index("Category"))

display(Markdown("### Agent Divergence"))
if not df_drift.empty:
    al_=df_drift["plan_code_alignment"].dropna()
    di_=df_drift["o_p_disagreement"].dropna()
    display(Markdown(f"""
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Samples with plan extracted | {len(al_)} |
| Mean O→P disagreement | **{di_.mean():.3f}** ({di_.mean()*100:.1f}%) |
| Std O→P disagreement | {di_.std():.3f} |
| Mean plan-code alignment | {al_.mean():.3f} |
| Nudge rate | {df_drift['nudge_triggered'].mean()*100:.1f}% |
"""))
else: display(Markdown("*No drift metadata available.*"))

display(Markdown("### Verbal-vs-Execution Divergence"))
if not df_drift.empty and n_both > 0:
    display(Markdown(f"""
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Samples with both scalars | {n_both} / {len(drift_rows)} ({100*n_both/max(1,len(drift_rows)):.0f}%) |
| Disagreements | **{n_dis} ({100*n_dis/max(1,n_both):.1f}%)** |
| Disagree + correct (execution won) | {len(exec_won) if n_dis else 0} |
| Disagree + wrong | {len(both_wrong) if n_dis else 0} |
"""))
else: display(Markdown("*No scalar extraction data.*"))

display(Markdown("### Nudge Recovery"))
display(Markdown(f"""
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Samples nudged | {n_nudged} / {n} ({100*n_nudged/max(1,n):.1f}%) |
| Recovered by nudge | **{n_recovered} ({100*n_recovered/max(1,n_nudged):.1f}% of nudged)** |
| Failed after nudge | {n_failed} |
"""))

display(Markdown("### Drift Onset (where available)"))
if not df_drift.empty and df_drift["drift_onset_turn"].notna().any():
    dt_=df_drift["drift_onset_turn"].dropna()
    dk_=df_drift["drift_onset_tokens"].dropna()
    ds_=df_drift["drift_onset_sec"].dropna()
    display(Markdown(f"""
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Samples that drifted (onset detected) | {len(dt_)} / {len(drift_rows)} ({100*len(dt_)/max(1,len(drift_rows)):.1f}%) |
| Mean drift onset — turn | {dt_.mean():.2f} |
| Mean drift onset — tokens | {dk_.mean():.0f} |
| Mean drift onset — seconds | {ds_.mean():.1f}s |
"""))

display(Markdown("### Key Anchoring Numbers (for trajectory figure)"))
if not df_traj.empty and "task_anchoring" in df_traj.columns:
    for phase in ["orchestrator","workflow"]:
        v=df_traj[df_traj["phase"]==phase]["task_anchoring"].dropna()
        if len(v): display(Markdown(f"- **{phase}** anchoring: mean={v.mean():.3f}, std={v.std():.3f}, n={len(v)}"))

print("\nAll numbers above can be cited directly in the report.")
print(f"Figures directory : {FIG_DIR}")
print(f"CSVs directory    : {OUT_DIR}")
